vocab.meaning: chi một khoản tiền lớn (thường là miễn cưỡng)
vocab.example: I'm not always keen to shell out hundreds of dollars just for a designer logo.

vocab.meaning: trả tiền cho cái gì đó đắt đỏ
vocab.example: I had to fork out a fortune for these limited-edition sneakers, but they were worth every penny.

vocab.meaning: mua nhanh chóng (khi có cơ hội hoặc giá rẻ)
vocab.example: Whenever Nike has a clearance sale, people flock to the stores to snap up the best deals.

vocab.meaning: vung tiền vào việc mua sắm xa xỉ
vocab.example: Occasionally, I like to splash out on a pair of high-end running shoes as a reward for my hard work.

vocab.meaning: phù hợp với, đi kèm với
vocab.example: I usually choose classic Converse because they go with almost any outfit in my wardrobe.

vocab.meaning: trì hoãn (chờ đợi dịp tốt hơn)
vocab.example: I'm holding off on buying those Adidas until the Black Friday sales begin.

vocab.meaning: mua dự trữ số lượng lớn
vocab.example: When I find a brand that fits perfectly, I tend to stock up on a few pairs in different colors.
I never buy shoes online because I need to try them on to ensure the arch support is adequate.

vocab.meaning: khảo giá, so sánh nhiều cửa hàng trước khi mua
vocab.example: It's wise to shop around before buying big brands, as prices can vary significantly between retailers.
Famous brands often invest in unique designs that help the wearer stand out in a crowd.
